M1 - Quiz 1.question: Which of the following is the BEST example of traditional AI, not Generative AI?
options: Writing a new short story about a robot teacher.
Composing a unique piece of music based on a prompt.
Suggesting the fastest driving route using Google Maps.
Generating a custom image of a historical scene.
M1 - Quiz 1.correct_answer: Writing a new short story about a robot teacher.

M1 - Quiz 1.question: According to the text, what is the key difference between Generative AI and a traditional search engine?
options: Generative AI is faster at finding information.
Generative AI can only work with images, not text.
Generative AI retrieves existing answers from the internet.
Generative AI generates new content based on learned patterns.
M1 - Quiz 1.correct_answer: Generative AI is faster at finding information.

M1 - Quiz 1.question: How does Generative AI learn to create new content?
options: By copying and pasting information from specific websites.
By being trained on massive amounts of data to recognize patterns.
By following a pre-programmed set of rules written by a computer scientist.
By asking users clarifying questions until it understands the task.
M1 - Quiz 1.correct_answer: By copying and pasting information from specific websites.

M1 - Quiz 1.question: The text mentions three primary ways AI can benefit educators. Which of the following is NOT one of them?
options: Saving time on administrative tasks
Replacing the need for classroom instruction.
Personalizing learning for different student needs.
Sparking creativity for both teachers and students.

M1 - Quiz 1.question: What is one of the key ethical concerns or responsibilities for educators when using AI, as mentioned in the text?
options: Ensuring the AI software is updated daily.
Teaching students to use AI to complete all their homework.
Guiding students to think critically and check the accuracy of AI-generated content.
Limiting AI use to only teachers and not allowing students to access it.
M1 - Quiz 1.correct_answer: Ensuring the AI software is updated daily.

M1 - Quiz 3.question: When using the CPTF prompting framework, which component most directly tells the AI how you want the final text to be structured (for example, “one short paragraph” or “two bulleted lists”)?
options: Context
Persona
Task
Format

M1 - Quiz 3.question: A teacher asks an AI: “Create a syllabus.” The AI returns a very generic syllabus. What is the single best step the teacher should take to get a more useful result?
options: Switch to a different AI model
Provide more specific prompt details (audience, duration, assessment style)
Ask for a summary of the generic syllabus
Reduce the prompt to a single word
M1 - Quiz 3.correct_answer: Switch to a different AI model

M1 - Quiz 3.question: Which prompt-writing habit most reduces the need for repeated fixes after the AI generates an initial output?
options: Keeping prompts very short to save time
Drafting a detailed prompt (including audience, objectives, format, and constraints) in a separate document before pasting it into the model
Always asking for the longest possible response
Only asking the AI to “use its best judgment”
M1 - Quiz 3.correct_answer: Keeping prompts very short to save time

M1 - Quiz 3.question: Suppose an instructor needs a course syllabus that aligns with Bloom’s revised taxonomy and includes a 100-mark project rubric. Which combination of prompt elements will most reliably produce that output?
options: Context: “Create a syllabus.” + no other details
Persona: “Act as a student.” + Task: “Write topics.”
Context: course and audience + Persona: expert curriculum designer + Task: include outcomes and rubric + Format: weekly table and rubric details
Format only: “Make a table.”
M1 - Quiz 3.correct_answer: Context: “Create a syllabus.” + no other details

M1 - Quiz 3.question: You want an AI to create a one-semester syllabus for an undergraduate marketing course. Which prompt revision will most likely produce the clearest, most usable output?
options: “Create a marketing syllabus.”
“Create a 15-week marketing syllabus with readings.”
“Create a syllabus for an introductory marketing course.”

“You are an expert curriculum designer with 30 years’ industry experience. Create a 15-week semester syllabus for first-year Bachelor of Commerce students. Include a 150-word course description, weekly topics, one case study per unit, suggested in-class activities, a 100-mark project with milestones and a rubric, and course outcomes aligned to Revised Bloom’s Taxonomy.”

M1 - Quiz 4.question: Why should educators prefer institution-approved AI tools instead of random public platforms?
options: They guarantee more creative results
They prevent bias in all outputs
They protect student data and ensure safer handling of educational content
They are always faster and free to use

M1 - Quiz 4.question: What is prompt hygiene in the context of teaching students to use AI tools responsibly?
options: Avoiding slang or abbreviations in prompts
Writing grammatically perfect instructions for AI
Excluding personal or identifying information when crafting prompts
Saving all prompts in a shared classroom database
M1 - Quiz 4.correct_answer: Avoiding slang or abbreviations in prompts

M1 - Quiz 4.question: Which statement should appear under the “Permitted Uses” section of a simple AI classroom policy?
options: “Students may use AI for brainstorming, grammar checking, and research summarization.”
“AI tools are strictly banned for all assignments.”
“AI may be used to complete and submit full assignments automatically.”
“Students must use only one AI tool chosen by the teacher.”
M1 - Quiz 4.correct_answer: “Students may use AI for brainstorming, grammar checking, and research summarization.”

M1 - Quiz 4.question: Which of the following is an effective strategy to assess student learning in an era of AI tools?
options: Evaluate only the final written submission
Require multiple drafts, peer reviews, and reflections to track learning progress
Use AI to grade all assignments automatically
Replace writing tasks with quizzes only
M1 - Quiz 4.correct_answer: Evaluate only the final written submission

M1 - Quiz 4.question: What practice best demonstrates responsible student use of AI in coursework?
options: Keeping AI use secret to avoid suspicion
Clearly citing which tools were used and what parts of the assignment they supported
Using AI for all portions of the assignment without supervision
Submitting AI-generated content as original work
M1 - Quiz 4.correct_answer: Keeping AI use secret to avoid suspicion

M1 - Quiz 5.question: Which statement best describes Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the context of education?
options: It is a set of hardware tools used to project visuals in classrooms.
It is software that can perform tasks requiring human intelligence, such as analyzing, reasoning, or problem-solving.
It is a system that stores large amounts of student data without processing it.
It is only used for entertainment and has no educational application.
M1 - Quiz 5.correct_answer: It is a set of hardware tools used to project visuals in classrooms.

M1 - Quiz 5.question: What makes Machine Learning (ML) different from traditional computer programming?
options: It follows fixed instructions written by a human for every task.
It cannot handle numerical data or patterns.
It learns from data and improves its performance without being explicitly programmed for every step.
It stops learning once trained.
M1 - Quiz 5.correct_answer: It follows fixed instructions written by a human for every task.

M1 - Quiz 5.question: Which of the following best defines an Algorithm?
options: A sequence of coded steps that helps solve a specific problem
A piece of hardware that speeds up AI processing
A random generator used to create ideas for teaching
A visual representation of classroom data
M1 - Quiz 5.correct_answer: A sequence of coded steps that helps solve a specific problem

M1 - Quiz 5.question: What is the main feature of Generative AI that makes it useful for teachers and students?
options: It only analyzes old data without creating anything new
It generates new, original content such as text, images, or summaries
It is limited to performing calculations
It only provides raw data for analysis
M1 - Quiz 5.correct_answer: It only analyzes old data without creating anything new

M1 - Quiz 5.question: How does Natural Language Processing (NLP) help in education?
options: It enables AI tools to understand and respond to human language, allowing smoother teacher–student communication through chat or text.
It translates computer code into binary numbers.
It focuses only on recognizing student handwriting.
It stores vocabulary lists without interpretation.
M1 - Quiz 5.correct_answer: It enables AI tools to understand and respond to human language, allowing smoother teacher–student communication through chat or text.

M2 - Quiz 1.question: How is Perplexity AI fundamentally different from a traditional search engine like Google?
options: It provides raw web links without explanations
It gives summarized, cited answers directly instead of multiple search results
It only searches for videos and podcasts
It requires external citation tools to verify information
M2 - Quiz 1.correct_answer: It provides raw web links without explanations

M2 - Quiz 1.question: Which of the following best describes the benefit of using Perplexity’s Academic Focus?
options: It increases the number of advertisements in results
It filters results from credible scholarly and journal-based sources
It summarizes fictional and entertainment-based content
It removes all citations to make responses shorter
M2 - Quiz 1.correct_answer: It increases the number of advertisements in results

M2 - Quiz 1.question: If a teacher wants Perplexity to find accurate mathematical or data-driven solutions, which Focus Mode would be most suitable?
options: YouTube Focus
Creative Writing Focus
Wolfram Alpha Focus
General Web Focus

M2 - Quiz 1.question: When using Perplexity for classroom preparation, what is the recommended best practice for educators?
options: Copy content directly without checking references
Always cite and verify information before sharing with students
Avoid using any academic sources
Rely only on general web summaries
M2 - Quiz 1.correct_answer: Copy content directly without checking references

M2 - Quiz 1.question: Which of the following statements captures Perplexity’s role for educators?
options: It replaces the need for teacher-led research entirely
It acts as a co-pilot that saves time by offering concise, referenced answers
It focuses mainly on entertainment-based searches
It only works for language and literature topics
M2 - Quiz 1.correct_answer: It replaces the need for teacher-led research entirely

M2 - Quiz 2.question: What are the three basic inputs required to generate a complete lesson plan using Eduaide.AI?
options: Grade, topic, and learning objectives
Subject, teacher’s name, and class strength
Time duration, school name, and syllabus
Course code, institution, and grading policy

M2 - Quiz 2.question: How does Eduaide.AI help teachers address the needs of diverse learners in a classroom?
options: By translating lesson plans into multiple languages
By creating differentiated content for varying ability levels
By automatically assigning students to groups
By replacing teacher-led instruction with pre-recorded videos
M2 - Quiz 2.correct_answer: By translating lesson plans into multiple languages

M2 - Quiz 2.question: Which of the following types of assessments can Eduaide.AI assist in creating?
options: Only multiple-choice quizzes
Only written essays
Both quizzes and project-based assessments
Only peer-reviewed assignments

M2 - Quiz 2.question: What is the main function of the Feedback Bot in Eduaide.AI?
options: To grade student assignments automatically
To provide suggestions for improving lesson structure and engagement
To calculate assessment scores and averages
To generate student attendance summaries

M2 - Quiz 2.question: Which of the following statements best captures the overall purpose of Eduaide.AI?
options: To replace teachers with automated AI-driven lessons
To serve as a co-creator that helps teachers plan, differentiate, assess, and refine lessons efficiently
To manage student discipline and administrative records
To generate advertisements for educational institutions
M2 - Quiz 2.correct_answer: To replace teachers with automated AI-driven lessons

M2 - Quiz 3.question: A teacher has long meeting notes from a parent-teacher session. They want a quick overview of the main discussion points before writing a reflection. Which Notion AI feature should they use?
options: Content Linking
AI Summarization
Project Outline
Table Database

M2 - Quiz 3.question: You’ve used Perplexity AI to research “Climate Change and Its Effects on Agriculture.” Now you want to include these findings in your lesson plan inside Notion. Which feature helps you connect both pieces of content seamlessly?
options: Project Timeline
Content Linking
Gallery View
Reminder Tool

M2 - Quiz 3.question: Ms. Rina is comparing her old Google Docs-based planning system with her new Notion AI workspace. She notices she spends less time managing files and more time refining lessons.  Which of the following best explains why Notion AI is more efficient?
options: It supports more fonts and themes
It combines notes, databases, and AI tools in one place
It works offline without internet
It limits the need for collaboration
M2 - Quiz 3.correct_answer: It supports more fonts and themes

M2 - Quiz 3.question: A group of teachers claims that using a shared Notion workspace can improve collaboration in their department. Which evidence best supports their claim?
options: Notion allows teachers to co-edit pages, databases, and share updates in real time.
Notion automatically grades students and uploads marks.
Notion restricts team access for data privacy.
Notion only allows one user to view each workspace.
M2 - Quiz 3.correct_answer: Notion allows teachers to co-edit pages, databases, and share updates in real time.

M2 - Quiz 3.question: Imagine you’re designing a Notion workspace for an upcoming “STEM Week” at school. You want to manage research, activities, and reflections efficiently. Which structure will make your workspace most effective?
options: One long text page with all details mixed together
Multiple linked pages for Research, Planning, Tracking, and Reflection
Separate folders stored outside Notion
A single table listing all student names only
M2 - Quiz 3.correct_answer: One long text page with all details mixed together

M3 - Quiz 2.question: 1. In Canva, which of the following tools helps you generate written content for worksheets, newsletters, or posters?
options: Magic Eraser
Magic Write
Magic Design
Magic Animate

M3 - Quiz 2.question: 2. What is the core difference between Magic Write and Magic Design?
options: Magic Write designs images, while Magic Design writes text.
Both tools perform the same function but in different file formats.
Magic Design focuses only on animation templates.
Magic Write handles text generation, while Magic Design creates visuals and layouts based on your ideas.
M3 - Quiz 2.correct_answer: Magic Write designs images, while Magic Design writes text.

M3 - Quiz 2.question: 3. What is the main advantage of using Canva’s “Text to Image” feature in classroom material design?
options: It finds free stock photos from the internet.
It automatically inserts videos from YouTube.
It allows educators to generate custom, unique images based on descriptive prompts.
It converts images into text descriptions.
M3 - Quiz 2.correct_answer: It finds free stock photos from the internet.

M3 - Quiz 2.question: 4. When writing prompts for “Text to Image,” what key element improves image quality?
options: Being specific about visual details like style, setting, and mood.
Keeping the description short and vague.
Avoiding artistic style references.
Including random keywords for variety.
M3 - Quiz 2.correct_answer: Being specific about visual details like style, setting, and mood.

M3 - Quiz 2.question: 5. Which Canva AI feature would be most useful for teachers communicating with multilingual parents?
options: Magic Translate
Magic Eraser
Magic Animate
Magic Write

M3 - Quiz 3.question: 1. You’re teaching a marketing class and want a visual showing “students presenting a business plan in a modern classroom.” The images generated look too generic and cartoonish. What’s the best way to improve your prompt?
options: Make it shorter so AI can “fill in the blanks.”
Remove adjectives to simplify the request.
Regenerate the same prompt multiple times until it looks right.
Add details like “three diverse college students presenting a marketing plan in a bright, modern classroom, realistic photo style.”
M3 - Quiz 3.correct_answer: Make it shorter so AI can “fill in the blanks.”

M3 - Quiz 3.question: 2. You want to create a historical image of “India’s Independence movement.” Which tool is better suited for creating a realistic and accurate visual?
options: Canva’s Text to Image, because it’s more artistic and playful.
Microsoft Designer, because it’s optimized for realistic, historically accurate visuals.
Either tool, since both create identical results.
Neither; AI cannot generate historical images.
M3 - Quiz 3.correct_answer: Canva’s Text to Image, because it’s more artistic and playful.

M3 - Quiz 3.question: 3. A professor types this prompt in Canva: “Students learning together.” The image that appears is vague and unclear. What’s the most effective fix?
options: Add specific context and tone, like “three college students studying business analytics in a library, 3D illustration style.”
Keep the same text but change the font color.
Use the Magic Write tool instead.
Add random keywords like “creative, amazing, bright.”
M3 - Quiz 3.correct_answer: Add specific context and tone, like “three college students studying business analytics in a library, 3D illustration style.”

M3 - Quiz 3.question: 4. A teacher compares Canva’s “Text to Image” with Microsoft Designer after generating classroom visuals. Which statement best describes their differences?
options: Canva produces realistic visuals; Microsoft Designer produces cartoon-like outputs.
Canva is better for creative, artistic designs; Microsoft Designer is better for realistic and structured images.
Both tools focus only on generating posters.
Canva works offline; Designer needs no internet connection.
M3 - Quiz 3.correct_answer: Canva produces realistic visuals; Microsoft Designer produces cartoon-like outputs.

M3 - Quiz 3.question: 5. A science lecturer wants to ensure AI visuals in her lessons show diversity and inclusivity. Which prompt demonstrates responsible and ethical AI use?
options: “Students conducting a chemistry experiment.”
“Science experiment in a lab.”
“Four diverse students, including one using a wheelchair, working together on a chemistry experiment in a bright lab, realistic photo style.”
“Any students doing science, style doesn’t matter.”

M3 - Quiz 4.question: 1. You’ve asked Magic Write to generate a 100-word introduction on “Entrepreneurial Mindset in College Students.” The paragraph feels generic and lacks real-world context. What’s the best way to improve it?
options: Regenerate the same prompt repeatedly until it feels better.
Add a command such as “Include a short example of a student-led startup on campus.”
Copy the text into another AI tool for editing.
Leave it as is, since AI can’t personalize content.
M3 - Quiz 4.correct_answer: Regenerate the same prompt repeatedly until it feels better.

M3 - Quiz 4.question: 2. When designing a worksheet in Canva Docs using Magic Write content, which of the following practices creates the most effective learning experience for students?
options: Keeping text minimal and balancing it with visual spacing, icons, or short instructions.
Filling the worksheet with as much text as possible to show depth.
Using long academic paragraphs to challenge students.
Adding multiple fonts and colors to make it “look busy.”
M3 - Quiz 4.correct_answer: Keeping text minimal and balancing it with visual spacing, icons, or short instructions.

M3 - Quiz 4.question: 3. A faculty member is creating an infographic summarizing “Ethical Marketing Principles.” What is the correct sequence of steps using Canva Docs and Magic Write?
options: Write content manually → export to Word → add visuals later.
Type prompt in Magic Write → generate key points → click “Convert to Design” → format infographic.
Create slides first → paste infographic later.
Generate random visuals → match text afterward.
M3 - Quiz 4.correct_answer: Write content manually → export to Word → add visuals later.

M3 - Quiz 4.question: 4. When using Magic Write for a parent newsletter, which of the following commands will help ensure your message sounds warm and approachable?
options: “Make it sound academic.”
“Add more jargon and complex words.”
“Keep the tone neutral and distant.”
“Rewrite this in a friendly and conversational tone.”

M3 - Quiz 4.question: 5. You’re combining AI text and visuals to create a one-page resource on “Sustainable Business Practices.” Which of the following approaches best uses the strengths of Canva Docs?
options: Generate a block of text and paste an image randomly beside it.
Use Magic Write for structured content, pair each section with a relevant AI-generated visual, and arrange them using Canva’s layout tools.
Generate multiple unrelated visuals to fill empty space.
Use only visuals; text makes the design too formal.
M3 - Quiz 4.correct_answer: Generate a block of text and paste an image randomly beside it.

M4 - Quiz 1.question: In RunwayML, which feature would you use to transform a written idea like “a fast-growing tree under sunlight” into a short animated clip?
options: Infinite Image
Text-to-Video
Motion Brush
Text-to-Speech

M4 - Quiz 1.question: What does the “Infinite Image” feature in RunwayML allow you to do?
options: Duplicate objects endlessly
Create infinite versions of the same video
Automatically loop a clip forever
Expand the background or scene of an image beyond its original borders

M4 - Quiz 1.question: The “Motion Brush” tool is designed to:
options: Animate specific elements within an image or video clip
Add text overlays or captions
Adjust brightness and color tones
Clean up unwanted backgrounds
M4 - Quiz 1.correct_answer: Animate specific elements within an image or video clip

M4 - Quiz 1.question: How can educators combine RunwayML with other AI tools from this course to create a complete learning experience?
options: Use RunwayML visuals with Synthesia avatar introductions and ElevenLabs voiceovers
Use RunwayML for text editing and grammar checking
Replace slides entirely with RunwayML clips
Use RunwayML only for background music creation
M4 - Quiz 1.correct_answer: Use RunwayML visuals with Synthesia avatar introductions and ElevenLabs voiceovers

M4 - Quiz 1.question: What is the main pedagogical benefit of using short, AI-generated video clips (5–15 seconds) as lesson hooks?
options: They replace lectures completely
They visually stimulate curiosity and help students connect emotionally to the topic before instruction
They fill unused class time
They make lessons longer but more detailed

M4 - Quiz 2.question: Compared to tools like RunwayML, what additional capability do Veo and Sora aim to provide?
options: 3D modeling for physical prototypes.
Rendering longer, more complex scenes with multiple camera angles.
Automatic lesson plan generation.
Voice synthesis from text.

M4 - Quiz 2.question: In an educational setting, how could Veo or Sora transform traditional video-based lessons?
options: By replacing teachers entirely.
By converting classroom recordings into podcasts.
By simplifying grading tasks.
By allowing educators to design immersive, story-driven learning experiences that feel cinematic

M4 - Quiz 2.question: Which of the following is a realistic classroom application of AI tools like Veo and Sora?
options: Generating a 3-minute simulation of a volcanic eruption for a geography lesson
Writing automatic student reports.
Translating essays into multiple languages.
Scheduling online meetings automatically.
M4 - Quiz 2.correct_answer: Generating a 3-minute simulation of a volcanic eruption for a geography lesson

M4 - Quiz 2.question: How does the “text-to-cinematic” capability of Veo and Sora differ from the “text-to-video” capability in RunwayML?
options: It uses simpler prompts.
It focuses on static camera angles only.
It generates complex, film-like storytelling sequences with dynamic perspectives.
It can only animate cartoon-like visuals.

M4 - Quiz 2.question: What new challenges will educators need to consider as they begin using advanced AI video tools?
options: Writing lesson plans manually.
Ethical use, authenticity of visuals, and verifying factual accuracy.
Installing expensive equipment.
Hiring professional editors.

M4 - Quiz 3.question: How can AI voice tools enhance accessibility in education?
options: By replacing teachers’ classroom instruction
By providing audio versions of readings for students with visual or reading difficulties
By removing the need for textbooks
By grading student work automatically

M4 - Quiz 3.question: Which of the following features allows teachers to make AI narrations sound more personalized and relatable?
options: Tone adjustment and voice style selection
Adding background images
Changing font size in transcripts
Limiting playback speed
M4 - Quiz 3.correct_answer: Tone adjustment and voice style selection

M4 - Quiz 3.question: What is one unique capability of advanced tools like ElevenLabs?
options: Cloning the teacher’s own voice for customized narrations
Translating live classroom discussions
Generating classroom attendance reports
Designing slides automatically
M4 - Quiz 3.correct_answer: Cloning the teacher’s own voice for customized narrations

M4 - Quiz 3.question: Why is tone variation important when creating AI-generated audio for lessons?
options: It helps the system pronounce words correctly
It makes the narration more emotionally engaging and easier to listen to
It speeds up the AI’s processing time
It improves the file compression ratio
M4 - Quiz 3.correct_answer: It helps the system pronounce words correctly

M4 - Quiz 3.question: What advantage does multilingual AI audio bring to global or diverse classrooms?
options: It simplifies grading systems
It enables lessons to be shared across different language groups
It creates written translations only
It replaces in-person interpreters entirely
